task: Classify customer product reviews as positive or negative to help an e-commerce business detect product problems early. Train a binary text classifier and evaluate it.
llm_decisions: Full 7-agent pipeline planned for end-to-end NLP classification task
Text cleaned (lowercase, punctuation removal) and meta features engineered
Dataset passed validation with 4 minor warnings (imbalance, 2 text duplicates, exclamation outliers, low feature count)
LogisticRegression selected over MultinomialNB: higher CV AUC (0.9609 vs 0.9602), Test F1 (0.9486 vs 0.9406), Test AUC (0.9646 vs 0.9627)
GridSearchCV selected C=5.0, class_weight=None as best hyperparameters
Model reviser verdict: PASS — F1 > 0.97, AUC > 0.99 exceed sentiment classification thresholds
key_findings: Numeric features have near-zero correlation with target (max r=0.034); primary signal is in review text
Class imbalance 3.2:1 (76% positive, 24% negative) — addressed via F1/AUC metric selection during tuning
Negative class recall = 89.4% — 506 false positives, 216 false negatives out of 20,000 reviews
ROC-AUC of 99.08% indicates near-perfect ranking ability
issues: Negative class recall 89.4% — 10.6% of complaints missed; consider lowering decision threshold
Evaluation dataset (clean.csv) may overlap with training data — metrics could be optimistic
Class imbalance not explicitly addressed (class_weight=None); consider class_weight='balanced' if recall is primary KPI
exclamation_count has 7.4% outliers (max=87) — consider capping at 99th percentile
